name: oha (HTTP Load Generator with TUI)

performance.notes: Rust + tokio; high-performance async HTTP client

agent_readiness.notes: Rust-based HTTP load generator with real-time TUI. oha -n 10000 -c 100 http://localhost:3000. -q rate (QPS). --no-tui for CI/scripts. -j for JSON output. Real-time terminal histogram, latency percentiles, RPS. HTTP/2 support. Similar to hey (Google) but with better UI and Rust performance. Colored histogram shows latency distribution live. --timeout flag. --redirect-limit. Installed via cargo or brew.

gotchas: TUI mode not suitable for CI (use --no-tui --json)
No complex scenario support (single endpoint only)
Cargo install required if not using brew
best_when: Quick HTTP endpoint benchmarking with beautiful live TUI
Developer laptop benchmarking to see real-time latency histogram
Rust ecosystem teams wanting native performance benchmarking
avoid_when: When vegeta's precise constant-rate control needed
Team standardized on k6 or locust
summary: oha is a Rust HTTP load generator with a beautiful real-time TUI showing latency histograms, percentiles, and RPS live. oha -n 10000 -c 100 http://target. -q for QPS rate control. HTTP/2 support. --no-tui --json for CI. Alternative to hey (Google) and wrk with better visual feedback. Great for developer-friendly quick benchmarks on any endpoint.
